message:
fix(conformance): surface JSON decode errors in resource fixture generator

Wrap json.loads in _read_resource_json with a try-except that converts
JSONDecodeError to a RuntimeError including the resource URI and parse
error details. Previously, a malformed JSON response from an MCP
resource would raise a bare JSONDecodeError with no indication of which
resource failed, making conformance test debugging unnecessarily hard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -354,7 +354,10 @@ async def _read_resource_json(mcp: Any, uri: str) -> Any:
     text = getattr(item, "content", None)
     if not isinstance(text, str):
         raise RuntimeError(f"resource returned non-text content: {uri}")
-    return json.loads(text)
+    try:
+        return json.loads(text)
+    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
+        raise RuntimeError(f"resource returned invalid JSON for {uri}: {e}")
 
 
 async def _generate() -> dict[str, Any]:
